hypothesis: Fine-tuning the pre-trained eSEN model with as few as 50 DFT-evaluated images per transition—explicitly including stress tensor targets and using a loss that weights stress errors twice as heavily as energy errors—will correct stress prediction biases enough to prevent symmetry-breaking artifacts and enable ML-only SSNEB to reproduce DFT-converged MEPs within 0.02 eV barrier error for at least 80% of tested solid-state phase transitions (including CsPbI3 and TiO2).
